name: Chinese Chicken Salad (Lunden)
recipeIngredient: 2 tablespoons natural-style peanut butter, or to taste
2 teaspoons light brown sugar, or less
1 tablespoon Asian sesame oil, or to taste
OR substitute long pasta of choice
4 cups romaine lettuce, rinsed and spun
1/4 cup chopped fresh cilantro, leaves only
recipeInstructions:
Preheat the oven to 350°F. Prepare the grill. Start heating water for pasta.
In a bowl, whisk together all ingredients for the dressing.
Spray the chicken lightly with the nonstick vegetable oil spray. Grill the chicken on a rack set 4 inches from the glowing coals for 6 to 8 minutes per side, or until it is just cooked through. Let the chicken stand for 10 minutes before thinly slicing it.
Cook the pasta and drain it well. Rinse with cold water, and drain again. Arrange the pasta in one layer in a large shallow baking pan that has been sprayed with the nonstick vegetable oil spray. Bake the pasta for 20 minutes, or until it is just golden. Season cappellini with salt if desired, and break it into 1/2-inch-length pieces.
In a large bowl, toss the lettuce, carrots, scallions, chicken, cappellini, and cilantro with the dressing.
